feedback.text: Ladies and Gentlemen, as a long-standing video player and EU citizen, I would like to raise a topic that is very close to my heart: fair management of digital games, in particular in terms of ownership, preservation and heritability. For 14 years, I have had a Steam account with a current market value of over EUR 19.000 (purchasing value: approx. EUR 6000). This sum is not just money, it represents hundreds of hours of leisure, memories, emotional stories and a personal collection that has real cultural and moral value for me. However, it is precisely this digital possession that is under severe threat. Many games disappear after some time simply because servers are shut down or online services are discontinued. Content that I have bought legally is suddenly inoperable, without compensation, without alternatives. This feels like digital expropriation. I therefore call on you to take the following points into account under the Digital Fairness Act: 1. Right to long-term use: When a game goes offline, the provider should be obliged to provide a playable version or to publish server tools. My purchased game should not suddenly be taken away, just because a company stops support. 2. Heritability of digital content: In the analogue world, it is natural to bequestered books or films. Digital games should also be treated as ownership, not as a temporary licence to use that ends with me. 3. Legal certainty on digital ownership: Consumers need to know: When they buy a digital product, it belongs to them, not just ‘borrowed on a temporary basis’. There is an urgent need for a fair and clear framework. Video games are an important cultural asset, creative, narrative, technical. They deserve the same protection as other cultural works. And as users we deserve fairness, respect and legal certainty in the digital space. I very much hope that the Digital Fairness Act will address these aspects and shape the legal framework in such a way that digital ownership is really worth something, now and in the future. Yours sincerely,
